name: Stir-Fried Rice Noodles
recipeInstructions:
1. Place rice noodles in a large bowl. Cover with water; soak for 45 minutes.
2. Cut the chicken into 1 1/2-inch by 1/3-inch strips.
3. Cut shrimp in half lengthwise; set aside.
4. Combine water, fish sauce, sugar, lime juice, paprika, and red pepper in a small bowl.
5. Reserve 1/4 of the bean sprouts for topping; combine the remaining bean sprouts and green onions.
6. Drain noodles.
7. Heat a wok over medium-high heat. Add oil and heat.
8. Add garlic; fry until garlic starts to brown.
9. Increase heat.
10. Add chicken; stir-fry until almost cooked, about 2 minutes.
11. Push chicken to one side of the wok.
12. Break egg into the wok. Stir quickly to break up the yolk and scramble the egg.
13. When the egg is set, mix it with the chicken.
14. Add drained noodles, shrimp, fish-sauce mixture, and 3 tablespoons peanuts.
15. Cook and stir over high heat for 2 to 3 minutes or until noodles are soft and most of the liquid is absorbed.
16. Add green-onion mixture; cook, stirring, for 1 more minute.
17. Spoon onto a heated platter. Sprinkle with reserved bean sprouts, then with remaining peanuts.
